preamble: The transformation of the voiceless alveolar affricate /ts/ to the alveolar lateral approximant /l/ is a documented phonetic shift, observed as a form of lenition where a more complex obstruent simplifies to a sonorant.
phoneticEffects: Lenition, simplification of affricate to approximant, change in manner of articulation from affricate to lateral approximant

examples.note: A common phonetic change in the Sotho-Tswana group of languages, where /ts/ can shift to /l/ or /d/.
